feedback.text: The Digital Fairness Act should also include the proposals of the “Stop Destroying Videogames” petition. Currently, manufacturers have the possibility of irreversibly disabling video games at any time. We buy a licence for a product, which is not clear to most consumers. Most consumers continue to think of the product and not a licence. Manufacturers then use this in examples such as Anthem and The Crew to easily stop the game at any point. However, in certain cases where there is no alternative and where the game has to function 100 % online, there is of course no alternative in most game studios making it easy and using online functionality to protect copying. As soon as your servers shut down due to running costs, the game is inseparable. We require companies to draw up an end of life plan. It provides that, in the case of online functionalities, when they are posted, and in the case of functions that are not online, the game must then remain in a position to play. After the end of life date, we do not expect any assistance from the manufacturer in case of incompatibilities arising from new hardware or software. We expect only the single player, for example, to remain playable and the deactivation of online services will be communicated early. These are just my personal opinions on the petition itself, giving more examples and more and sharing better solutions. https://eci.ec.europa.eu/045/public/#/screen/home/disabled https://citizens-initiative.europa.eu/initiatives/details/2024/000007_en#
